title: Training random forest model using fecal biomarkers for assessing autism spectrum disorder
abstract: Described herein are systems, methods, and other techniques for training and deploying a random forest model to predict a presence of ASD in a subject. A training dataset can be obtained including abundance measurements of a first cohort of subjects with ASD and a second cohort of subjects without ASD across the set of microbial markers. Multiple candidate random forest models are trained by generating a validation dataset comprising one of k subsets of the training dataset and a training subset comprising the remaining k-1 subsets, training a candidate random forest model of the k candidate random forest models using the training subset, and assessing the candidate random forest model using the validation dataset by computing performance metrics for the candidate random forest model. Candidate random forest models are evaluated by computing an AUC.
